page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-48 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server configuration does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage. There are no integrated GPUs, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or deep learning workloads. Additionally, it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the c4a-standard-48 is designed for workloads requiring consistent compute performance without the extreme memory demands of memory-optimized instances. It is suitable for multi-threaded applications, web serving, containerized microservices, and medium-scale databases.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-48 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine instance within the c4a family. It is equipped with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on external storage options for data persistence. Designed for workloads requiring predictable compute performance, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures stable execution. The c4a-standard-48 is well-suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, containerized microservices, and database deployments that benefit from a balanced resource profile without specialized hardware acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-48 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM, designed for balanced enterprise workloads without local storage or GPUs.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and one hundred ninety-two gigabytes of memory for general-purpose workloads.
